result.instructions: Read-only access to the RisqBase Design System: components, tokens (light+dark), recipes, lifecycle, a11y contracts, scanner rules, and Layer-3 showcase entries (which are RALIA-private and not consumable). Prefer existing primitives and semantic tokens over custom builds; run search first when unsure.

      "description": "Nine variants resolve through the `citation.*` token chain (v4.3 Â§4.2). The variant changes the surface tint, the border treatment, and the glyph â€” but the *footprint* is constant so chips line-wrap predictably in flowing prose. v4.3 Â§5.2, closes RALIA F-012 / F-025. v2.1.0 (Spec 05 Actions B + C): - Action B: aria-label combines variant description + visible label so SR users hear \"Verified source: Art. 35(3)(c)\" not just \"Verified source\". The sr-only suffix span was removed because its content is now in the aria-label (avoids double-announcement). - Action C (WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size â€” Minimum): the interactive chip bumps to py-1 (~26px tall) to clear the 24Ă—24 minimum. Static (non-clickable) chips keep py-0.5 (~22px) for in-line typographic rhythm â€” they are not interactive controls and 2.5.8 does not apply. Gated on `typeof onOpen === 'function'`."

      "description": "v4.3 Â§5.3, status `beta`. A11y (brief F.3): every type renders a visually-hidden data table (`sr-only`) linked from the `<figure role=\"img\">` via `aria-describedby`, so screen-reader users can read the underlying series / cell values instead of relying on the `aria-label` summary alone."

{
  "name": "CitationChip",
  "domain": "ai",
  "layer": 2,
  "state": "stable",
  "exports": [
    "CitationChip"
  ],
  "description": "Nine variants resolve through the `citation.*` token chain (v4.3 Â§4.2). The variant changes the surface tint, the border treatment, and the glyph â€” but the *footprint* is constant so chips line-wrap predictably in flowing prose. v4.3 Â§5.2, closes RALIA F-012 / F-025. v2.1.0 (Spec 05 Actions B + C): - Action B: aria-label combines variant description + visible label so SR users hear "Verified source: Art. 35(3)(c)" not just "Verified source". The sr-only suffix span was removed because its content is now in the aria-label (avoids double-announcement). - Action C (WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size â€” Minimum): the interactive chip bumps to py-1 (~26px tall) to clear the 24Ă—24 minimum. Static (non-clickable) chips keep py-0.5 (~22px) for in-line typographic rhythm â€” they are not interactive controls and 2.5.8 does not apply. Gated on `typeof onOpen === 'function'`.",
  "import": "import { CitationChip } from '@risqbase-inc/ui-components/ai'",
  "api": [
    {
      "name": "CitationVariant",
      "kind": "type",
      "definition": "| 'verified' | 'pending' | 'low-confidence' | 'retracted' | 'external' | 'multi-source' | 'ai-inferred' | 'retrieval-failed' | 'user-pinned'"
    },
    {
      "name": "CitationChipProps",
      "kind": "interface",
      "members": [
        {
          "name": "variant",
          "type": "CitationVariant",
          "required": true
        },
        {
          "name": "label",
          "type": "ReactNode",
          "required": true,
          "description": "Visible label, e.g. "EU AI Act Art. 6" or "[3]". When passed as a string the value is also folded into the chip's accessible name ("Verified source: EU AI Act Art. 6"). When passed as a non-string ReactNode (e.g. JSX), the consumer should also pass an explicit `aria-label` so the chip remains self-describing to SR users."
        },
        {
          "name": "sourceCount",
          "type": "number",
          "required": false,
          "description": "For variant='multi-source': how many sources back the citation. Renders as a small count pill and is appended to the accessible name ("Multiple sources: Art. 30, 3 sources")."
        },
        {
          "name": "onOpen",
          "type": "() => void",
          "required": false,
          "description": "Optional click handler â€” opens the source / preview. Presence of this prop is what makes the chip interactive; interactive chips render as a `<button>` and use the larger `py-1` padding (WCAG 2.5.8)."
        },
        {
          "name": "aria-label",
          "type": "string",
          "required": false,
          "description": "Optional accessible description override. When unset, the chip computes its own from `variantDescription[variant] + ': ' + label` (v2.1.0 Spec 05 Action B)."
        },
        {
          "name": "className",
          "type": "string",
          "required": false
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "tokenChain": "# Tokens â€” `CitationChip`

Resolves through the `citation-chip.*` component-tier tokens, which wrap the semantic `citation.*` chain.

| Token | Used for |
|---|---|
| `color.citation-chip.surface` | default surface |
| `color.citation-chip.surface-low-conf` | `low-confidence`, `retrieval-failed` surface |
| `color.citation-chip.border` | default border |
| `color.citation-chip.border-retracted` | `retracted` border |
| `color.citation-chip.text` | default text |
| `color.citation-chip.text-retracted` | `retracted`, `retrieval-failed` text |
| `color.citation-chip.icon` | glyph |
| `color.citation-chip.hover` | hover state overlay (only when `onOpen`) |
| `color.citation-chip.active` | pressed state overlay |
| `color.iris.accent-subtle` | `ai-inferred` surface, halo |
| `color.iris.accent` | `ai-inferred` / `user-pinned` border |
| `dimension.radius.sm` | chip radius (4px â€” compact for line-flow) |
",
  "accessibilityContract": "# CitationChip â€” accessibility

A chip that surfaces the provenance / confidence of an AI-generated claim. Without it, the user can't tell verified content from inference.

## Contracts

### Accessible name (v2.1.0 Spec 05 Action B)

The chip's accessible name combines the variant description with the visible label content. SR users hear the trust framing first ("Verified source", "AI-inferred connection", "Pinned by user") then the citation content. This is the inverse of sighted reading order â€” visual prominence carries the framing implicitly via colour + border; SR must carry it lexically.

| Variant + label | SR announcement |
|---|---|
| `verified` Â· "Art. 35(3)(c)" | "Verified source: Art. 35(3)(c)" |
| `pending` Â· "EDPB 04/2026" | "Verification pending: EDPB 04/2026" |
| `ai-inferred` Â· "implied by ROPA 12" | "AI-inferred (no citation): implied by ROPA 12" |
| `multi-source` Â· "Art. 30" with `sourceCount=3` | "Multiple sources: Art. 30, 3 sources" |
| `retracted` Â· "Art. 35(3)(c)" | "Retracted source: Art. 35(3)(c)" |
| `user-pinned` Â· "Internal memo 2026-Q1" | "Pinned by user: Internal memo 2026-Q1" |

### Target size (v2.1.0 Spec 05 Action C â€” WCAG 2.5.8)

Interactive chips (those with `onOpen` provided) render with `py-1` for a rendered height of ~26px, clearing the 24Ă—24 minimum target-size requirement. Static chips (no `onOpen`) keep `py-0.5` (~22px) â€” they are not interactive controls and WCAG 2.5.8 does not apply.

### Element + role

- When `onOpen` is passed, the chip renders as a `<button type="button">` with the computed accessible name as `aria-label`.
- When `onOpen` is omitted, the chip renders as a bare `<span>` with the same `aria-label` â€” read by screen readers without becoming a tab stop. (v2.1.1 G4 FU-10 / CC-1: the `role="note"` previously applied to the non-interactive variant was removed; `note` is an ARIA 1.2 landmark and a long prose passage with 20+ inline chips would pollute landmark-jump navigation in NVDA / JAWS. The chip remains announced by AT via the `aria-label`.)
- The visual glyph is `aria-hidden="true"`.
- The multi-source count pill is `aria-hidden="true"` because the count is already part of the accessible name.
- `retracted` content is styled with `line-through` â€” but the meaning is also surfaced via the accessible name ("Retracted source: â€¦"), satisfying WCAG 1.4.1 (Use of Color).
- Pass a custom `aria-label` to override the computed name when the label alone is ambiguous, or when the label is a non-string ReactNode (e.g. JSX containing icons).

### Non-string labels

When `label` is a non-string ReactNode, only the variant description goes into the accessible name; the label content is omitted from the announcement. Always pass an explicit `aria-label` in this case so the chip remains self-describing.

### Duplicate-string edge case

For variants whose semantics imply unavailability (`retracted`, `retrieval-failed`), pass the original citation label (e.g. "Art. 35(3)(c)") when possible. The variant description is added automatically by the chip; passing redundant text in `label` (e.g. `<CitationChip variant="retrieval-failed" label="Source unavailable" />`) causes harmless double-announcement ("Retrieval failed: Source unavailable"). Tolerated but not preferred.

### G4 FU-14 (CC-2) â€” `ai-inferred` dashed-border legibility note (v2.1.1 sweep, 2026-05-20)

The `ai-inferred` variant uses a 1px dashed border. On high-DPI displays (retina,
Surface, modern Android phones) Chromium and Safari sub-pixel-anti-alias 1px
dashed strokes which can blur the dash gaps and read as solid at default zoom.
Verified manually at 100% browser zoom on:

- macOS Safari 18 retina â€” dashes visible
- macOS Chrome 134 retina â€” dashes visible
- iOS Safari 18 (iPhone 15, 3Ă— DPR) â€” dashes visible
- Windows 11 Chrome non-retina @ 1.0Ă— DPR â€” dashes visible

If a future browser refresh blurs the pattern, swap to `border-dotted` (slightly
chunkier) or `border-2 border-dashed` (defeats the in-line typographic rhythm â€”
last resort).

## Don't

- Don't strip `onOpen` to make the chip "not clickable" â€” pass `onOpen={undefined}` (omit the prop entirely) so the rendered element matches the intent (and the target-size + padding match).
- Don't re-introduce an `sr-only` suffix span repeating the variant description; it would double-announce now that the description is in the `aria-label`.
",
  "composesWith": [
    "TelemetryBeacon"
  ],
  "storybook": "src/ai/CitationChip/CitationChip.stories.tsx",
  "canonicalSnippet": "import { CitationChip } from '@risqbase-inc/ui-components/ai'",
  "_meta": {
    "spec": "v4.4",
    "package": "2.1.3",
    "generatedAt": "2026-06-10T00:23:06.830Z"
  }
}
